Industry news

Baking Paper Packaging News

Daily buyer-focused updates on greaseproof paper, baking cups, air fryer paper liners, food-contact paper packaging, PFAS-free materials and EUDR compliance.

Ask for Product SupportRead Buyer Guides

Buyer intelligence

What global food paper buyers should watch this week

These short updates are selected for importers, distributors, bakery packaging buyers and foodservice packaging teams. Each note explains why the topic matters for sourcing, compliance or product development.

2026-06-25PFAS-free paper

1. PFAS-free grease-barrier papers move from niche option to mainstream food packaging requirement

Food and pet-food packaging buyers are paying closer attention to how PFAS-free grease-barrier papers perform at folded edges, cut edges and converted formats. The practical message for converters is clear: barrier paper selection must be matched with packaging design, cutting and forming details.

Buyer takeaway: Ask suppliers to confirm PFAS-free test reports, edge performance and whether the paper is suitable for the exact format, such as burger wraps, liners, baking cups or formed trays.
Source: UPM Specialty Materials
2026-06-25EU regulation

2. EU PFAS limits for food-contact packaging make August 2026 a key compliance date

The EU Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation is pushing food-contact packaging buyers to review PFAS limits, packaging components and supplier documentation before the 12 August 2026 application date.

Buyer takeaway: For paper food packaging, buyers should review paper, coating, ink and adhesive documentation together rather than checking the base paper alone.
Source: Food Safety Magazine
2026-06-25EUDR

3. EUDR keeps traceability high on the agenda for paper and pulp packaging buyers

The EU Deforestation Regulation requires covered products placed on or exported from the EU to be deforestation-free and traceable. For paper packaging buyers, this makes paper sourcing data and due-diligence support increasingly important.

Buyer takeaway: When sourcing paper packaging for Europe, confirm whether the paper is covered by EUDR-related data systems and whether supplier documentation can support importer due diligence.
Source: European Commission
2026-06-25EUDR

4. Paper, pulp and printing companies prepare for heavier EUDR data collection

EUDR preparation is pushing companies in paper, pulp and printing to collect supplier and raw-material origin data more systematically. Manual document handling is becoming harder as buyers expect clearer traceability.

Buyer takeaway: Choose suppliers that can organize paper sourcing information, certificate files and product documentation in a way that buyers can actually review.
Source: osapiens
2026-06-25EUDR

5. Pulp and paper packaging faces EUDR due-diligence pressure through 2026 and 2027

EUDR guidance for pulp and paper highlights traceability, geolocation data, risk assessment and due-diligence statements. Large and medium enterprises face earlier pressure than small and micro companies.

Buyer takeaway: For European food packaging projects, importers should ask for EUDR-related support early, before confirming mass production or retail launch schedules.
Source: TraceX Technologies
2026-06-25Market trend

6. Baking and cooking paper market forecast points to long-term growth

Market forecasts indicate continued growth for baking and cooking paper, supported by bakery applications, home baking, foodservice and convenience cooking formats.

Buyer takeaway: Baking paper, cupcake liners and release papers are not short-term seasonal products only; they are part of a broader food preparation and bakery packaging growth category.
Source: Future Market Insights
2026-06-25Air fryer liners

7. Air fryer paper liners remain a high-velocity consumable category

Forecasts for air fryer paper liners point to sustained demand tied to appliance adoption, convenience cooking and reduced cleaning needs in home and commercial kitchens.

Buyer takeaway: Retail and ecommerce buyers should confirm shape, size, perforation, heat resistance, warning labels and packaging format before placing bulk orders.
Source: IndexBox
2026-06-25Food contact paper

8. Food contact paper market growth reinforces demand for verified paper packaging suppliers

Market analysis shows food contact paper remains a large and growing packaging category, with demand supported by takeaway, bakery, quick-service restaurant, retail food and sustainability trends.

Buyer takeaway: Buyers should look beyond low unit price and verify food-contact documentation, odor control, printing quality and stable carton delivery.
Source: Fortune Business Insights
2026-06-25Food contact materials

9. EU food-contact material discussion expands beyond plastics to inks, coatings and adhesives

Food-contact material policy discussions increasingly cover the full packaging system, including coatings, printing inks, adhesives and other non-plastic components used with food packaging.

Buyer takeaway: For printed greaseproof paper, baking cups and bags, buyers should review ink, coating and adhesive declarations along with the paper itself.
Source: European Parliament Research Service
2026-06-25Packaging regulation

10. PPWR shifts packaging buyer focus toward design, sustainability and documentation

The EU Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation brings new requirements that affect packaging design, sustainability claims and producer obligations. Food packaging buyers are expected to ask suppliers for clearer technical and compliance support.

Buyer takeaway: For bakery and foodservice paper packaging, prepare product specifications, packaging structure, material declarations and test reports before buyer approval.
Source: Gleiss Lutz
